design_philosophy.core_loop: Destroy designated enemy targets to accumulate energy for opening interdimensional portals; manage power-ups and projectile levels across sequential planet stages
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure after portal opens (10-second window to enter before body destruction); power level degradation on damage creates risk-reward for aggressive play
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Cybernetically enhanced martial artist pursuing killer across frontier worlds; evolved futuristic street fighting with energy projectiles and biomechanical augmentation
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Reframes Street Fighter IP as sci-fi action-platformer rather than fighting game; emphasizes movement mastery (wall climbing, flip jumps, ledge manipulation) over combat combos; power curve management is core progression metric

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives system; losing a life resets Ken to starting power level and respawns on current stage
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing; failure to enter portal within 10-second window after opening costs a life and forces stage restart
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player controls power accumulation speed by selectively destroying enemies and containers; can manage risk via aggressive vs. cautious approach to enemy engagement

progression.upgrade_system: Power-up capsule system: collecting 2 capsules increases projectile level (max 5 levels) increasing range and damage; power decreases by 1 on damage, resets to 0 on life loss. Additional power-ups: backup option for extra firepower, flip shield capsule for mid-air damage
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stages unlock sequentially after accumulating required enemy-drop energy; some stages require destroying multiple designated targets; later stages hint at obtainable upgrades (e.g., flip shield capsule on sand planet)
replayability_hooks: Five power levels encourage multiple playthroughs to master high-damage builds
Multiple enemy targets per stage allow routing variation
Projectile angle variety (horizontal, vertical, curved, diagonal) enables skill expression

world_building_techniques: Text scrawl opening establishing scientific backstory and revenge motivation
Stage-by-stage dialogue revealing plot twists (mysterious pursuer identity, Cyboplasm parasitic nature)
Gameplay reveals: discovering flip shield mechanics mirrors plot point of Ken/Kevin discovering hidden upgrades
Environmental context: frontier planets visited by killer create spatial exploration of criminal trail

boss_design.philosophy: Final confrontation against Troy/Dr. Jose serves as climactic reveal that lab partner faked death and implanted protagonist with Cyboplasm; boss encounter appears to test mastery of accumulated power-ups and movement techniques
notable_bosses: Troy (US version) / Dr. Jose (JP version) - Final antagonist who staged own death, stole Cyboplasm, implanted protagonist with parasitic substance; reveals he created the 'Parasite' enemies across frontier worlds

faction_indicators: Galaxy Police (Japan) vs. unaffiliated scientist Ken (US) - localization shift alters protagonist allegiance
Parasite/Cyboplasm-enhanced criminals as organized threat across frontier planets
danger_signals: Cyboplasm pandemic spreading across galaxy after theft
Mysterious entity taunting protagonist during pursuit suggests internal threat
Pain in protagonist's body signaling parasitic infection indicates existential bodily horror
environmental_storytelling: Each frontier planet contains traces of Cyboplasm spread by killer, marking trail of destruction
Planetary environments (sand planets, etc.) provide context for hidden upgrade locations
Lab setting establishes scientific origin of crisis before protagonist's journey begins

sources_used: tcrf - Street Fighter 2010: The Final Fight (The Cutting Room Floor)
wikipedia - Street Fighter 2010: The Final Fight
